paper_title: Aero Palynological Monitoring and Allergic Sensitization Study in Bratislava

methods.description: This method involves monitoring daily mean pollen concentrations during the vegetation period using a Burkard 7-day volumetric pollen trap to understand pollen distribution and its potential impact on allergies.

steps.description: The daily mean pollen concentrations were monitored during the vegetation period, February - October, in 2002 and 2003, using a Burkard 7-day volumetric pollen trap.
steps.searchable_summary: Monitor daily mean pollen concentrations using a Burkard 7-day volumetric pollen trap

steps.description: Sampler drums were changed weekly, and the tapes cut into 48 mm segments representing the previous 7 days.
steps.searchable_summary: Change sampler drums weekly and cut tapes into 48 mm segments

steps.description: To count the pollen grains in a daily sample, the method of 12-traverse transects was used.
steps.searchable_summary: Count pollen grains using 12-traverse transects method

steps.description: The pollen grains were identified according to a pollen atlas, using an optical microscope at magnification × 400.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify pollen grains using a pollen atlas and optical microscope at ×400 magnification

methods.description: This method involves determining specific IgE antibodies against pollen allergen extracts using an Immuno-CAP system to assess allergic sensitization in patients.

steps.description: A blood sample was taken to determine sIgE antibodies against 8 pollen allergen extracts.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect blood sample to determine sIgE antibodies against pollen allergens

steps.description: Specific IgE antibodies were determined using an Immuno-CAP system during 2002-2003.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine specific IgE antibodies using Immuno-CAP system

steps.description: The specific IgE level was classified using CAP-RAST scores.
steps.searchable_summary: Classify specific IgE levels using CAP-RAST scores

methods.description: This method uses statistical procedures to analyze the relationship between pollen concentrations, pollen season length, and median sIgE values.

steps.description: Non-parametric Spearman's correlation coefficients were used to establish the relationship between the annual total pollen concentrations, as well as the length of the main pollen season of selected pollen taxa and median sIgE values in 2002 and 2003.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Spearman's correlation coefficients between pollen data and sIgE values

steps.description: The SPSS 15.0 Software Package was used for all statistical analyses.
